---
title: "Always set swapon on ubuntu load"
date: 2008-01-26
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by Polemistis on 2008-01-26
At the moment I set swapon manually each time ubuntu loads.
I have searched the other threads, but it is not clear.

At the moment I do:

```
sudo swapon /dev/hda5
```

But I want to make the partition swap automatically when ubuntu loads. How can I do this?
I think I need to edit my fstab file. This is how it looks at the moment:

```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>
proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=b9efaee9-0f84-4ee2-8c4e-77d52a8bbd28 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
UUID=19c3c073-13e7-46d1-b2fa-d8e7162cc092 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/hda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hda2 /media/hda2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hdb1 /media/zbackup ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
```

Could someone tell me what to add and where pls

---

### Post by taurus on 2008-01-26
Check the UUID for /dev/hda5 to make sure it matches with the one in /etc/fstab.

```
ls -la /dev/disk/by-uuid
```

---

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-01-26
Edit your fstab file to make it look like this:-
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>
proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=b9efaee9-0f84-4ee2-8c4e-77d52a8bbd28 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
[COLOR="Red"]**/dev/hda5 none swap sw 0 0**[/COLOR]
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/hda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hda2 /media/hda2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hdb1 /media/zbackup ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
```

Your problem should be solved after that.

---

### Post by Polemistis on 2008-01-26

> Edit your fstab file to make it look like this:-
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>
proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=b9efaee9-0f84-4ee2-8c4e-77d52a8bbd28 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
[COLOR="Red"]**/dev/hda5 none swap sw 0 0**[/COLOR]
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/hda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hda2 /media/hda2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hdb1 /media/zbackup ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_GB.UTF-8 0 0
```

Your problem should be solved after that.

Thanks that fixed it.
